concerns.text: Task T012 mandates 'TinyLlama-1.1B' as the primary model. FR-002 and A-003 explicitly restrict authorized models to 'CodeLlama-7B, StarCoder, or Phi-2'. Selecting TinyLlama is a semantic weakening of the functional requirement's model scope, violating the constraint that the system MUST use one of the specified CPU-tractable models.

Task T014 implements stability checks using 'IQR of 3 runs; re-run if IQR > 15%'. Assumption A-012 in the spec mandates a 'coefficient of variation (CV)' check with a threshold of '> 0.1'. The task silently replaces the spec's required statistical metric (CV) with a different one (IQR), violating the constraint preservation principle for measurement methodology.

Task T021 implements 'Kaplan-Meier estimator' as the primary method for censored data. FR-004 explicitly states the system MUST apply 'Tobit regression (or Kaplan-Meier)' with Tobit listed as the primary option, and only fall back to KM if unavailable. The task assumes KM is the primary implementation without the Tobit-first constraint, weakening the requirement hierarchy.

Task T030 implements 'memory_per_loc' as a 'descriptive metric only'. FR-006 requires calculating this metric but does not restrict its use to descriptive purposes only; User Story 3 aims to 'analyze their correlation with memory consumption patterns'. Limiting the metric to 'descriptive only' silently narrows the analytical scope defined in the spec.

Task T015 records timeouts as '7.001 GB'. Assumption A-011 states execution exceeding 60s is recorded as 'timeout' and treated as execution failure (memory = 7.001 GB). While the value matches, the task description says 'record as timeout (7.001 GB)' which conflates the status label with the value. The spec requires recording the status as 'timeout' and the value as 7.001 GB separately for proper statistical handling (censored data). This risks semantic drift in data schema.
